blocks:
Surah Al-Muddaththir

Meccan, and it is 56 verses

Surah Al-Muddaththir

Meccan, its number of verses is fifty-six according to the Iraqi count, and fifty-five according to the Shami count. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) يا أَيُّهَا الْمُدَّثِّرُ ** (O you wrapped up [in your clothes]!) Meaning the one who is wrapped up (المتدثّر), and he is the wearer of the outer garment (الدّثار) (or) the shirt (القّمي). He said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم wrapped Himself up. So al-Muddaththir means the one wrapped up in his garment. It is narrated that He said: "I was in Hira', and I was called out to. So I looked to my right and my left, but I saw nothing. Then I looked above me, and behold, He was upon a throne between the heaven and the earth" (meaning the Angel who called out to Him). "So I was terrified, and I returned to Khadijah and said, 'Wrap me up!'" Then Gabriel (peace be upon him) descended and said: يا أَيُّهَا الْمُدَّثِّرُ. (O you wrapped up [in your clothes]!) And in al-Majma', there is what is close to this, with additions.

(٢) قُمْ فَأَنْذِرْ (2) Arise and warn! (٣) وَرَبَّكَ فَكَبِّرْ ** (3) and magnify your Lord, Describe Him with greatness (الكبرياء) both in belief (عقداً) and in word (قولاً). It is narrated: When the command to magnify (Him) (كبر) was revealed, he became certain that it was the Revelation (الوحي), because Satan does not command that. (٤) وَثِيابَكَ فَطَهِّرْ. (4) and purify your garments, In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: Meaning, so tuck them up (فَشَمِّرْ). And in a narration: He says, raise them and do not drag them. And from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said to His Prophet: وَثِيابَكَ فَطَهِّرْ ** (and purify your garments). And His garments were pure, and He only commanded him to tuck them up (بالتّشمير). And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Its meaning is, so shorten your garments (فَقَصِّرْ). And from him (al-Sadiq), narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon them both), he said: Washing garments removes worry (الهمّ) and grief (الحزن), and it is a purification (طهور) for the prayer, and tucking up (تشمير) the garments is a purification (طهور) for it. And Allah, the Glorified, has said: وَثِيابَكَ فَطَهِّرْ ** (and purify your garments), meaning: so tuck them up (فَشَمِّرْ). And al-Qummi (said): Their purification (تطهيرها) is their tucking up (تشميرها). And it is said: Our followers (شيعتنا) purify themselves.

(٥) وَالرُّجْزَ فَاهْجُرْ ** (5) and keep away from uncleanness. al-Qummi (القّمي) (said): al-Rijz (الرّجز) is the foul (الخبيث). And it was recited with a dammah (بالضمّ), and this is a linguistic variant (لغة فيه) of it. (٦) وَلا تَمْنُنْ تَسْتَكْثِرُ. (6) And do not grant a favour seeking to receive more. al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Do not give a gift (العطيّة) seeking more than it. And in al-Kafi (الكافي), narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said regarding this Ayah: Do not consider great what you have done of good for Allah. (٧) وَلِرَبِّكَ فَاصْبِرْ ** (7) And be patient for the sake of your Lord. (Be patient) regarding the hardships (مشاقّ) of the religious obligations (التكليف) and the harm (أذى) caused by the polytheists (المشركين). (٨) فَإِذا نُقِرَ فِي النَّاقُورِ ** (8) When the Trumpet is sounded, When the Horn (الصور) is blown (نفخ). (٩) فَذلِكَ يَوْمَئِذٍ يَوْمٌ عَسِيرٌ. (9) that will be a hard day then, (١٠) عَلَى الْكافِرِينَ غَيْرُ يَسِيرٍ ** (10) for the faithless, not easy. An emphasis (تأكيد) that suggests its ease (يسره) upon the believers (المؤمنين). And in al-Kafi (الكافي), narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, he said: "Indeed, among us is a victorious (مظفّراً), hidden (مستتراً) Imam. When Allah wills to manifest His affair, He places a mark (نكتة) in his heart, so he appears and establishes the command of Allah."

(١١) ذَرْنِي وَمَنْ خَلَقْتُ وَحِيداً ** (11) Leave Me and him whom I created alone. It was said that it was revealed concerning Al-Walid ibn Al-Mughirah, the uncle of Abu Jahl, for he was nicknamed al-Wahid (الوحيد). Allah named him thus in mockery (تهكّماً). And it was said, meaning: ذَرْنِي ** (Leave Me) alone with him, for I shall suffice you against him. And in al-Majma', narrated from Al-Baqir عليه السلام: al-Wahid (الوحيد) is he whose father is not known to him.

(١٢) وَجَعَلْتُ لَهُ مالاً مَمْدُوداً ** (12) and granted him extensive wealth, extensive (مبسوطاً), abundant (كثيراً). (١٣) وَبَنِينَ شُهُوداً ** (13) and sons present [with him], present (حضوراً) with him in Mecca, enjoying meeting them. (١٤) وَمَهَّدْتُ لَهُ تَمْهِيداً ** (14) and smoothed the way for him thoroughly. And I expanded for him in leadership (الرّياسة) and wide influence (الجاه العريض), until he was nicknamed the Basil of Quraysh (ريحانة قريش) and the Unique One (الوحيد). (١٥) ثُمَّ يَطْمَعُ أَنْ أَزِيدَ ** (15) Yet he is eager that I should increase [it]! beyond what he was given, and this is deeming his greed far-fetched (استبعاد لطمعه). (١٦) كَلَّا إِنَّهُ كانَ لِآياتِنا عَنِيداً. (16) No indeed! He has been defiant toward Our signs. (١٧) سَأُرْهِقُهُ صَعُوداً ** (17) Soon I shall impose on him a hard ascent. I shall cover him with a difficult ascent (عقبة شاقّة المصعد), and this is a metaphor for the hardships (الشدائد) he will encounter. And it is narrated: that al-Sa'ud (الصعود) is a mountain of fire which he will ascend for seventy autumns (خريفاً), then he will fall therein likewise forever (أبداً). And in a narration: when he places his hand upon it, it melts, and when he lifts it, it returns, and likewise his leg.

** إِنَّهُ فَكَّرَ وَقَدَّرَ ** (18) He reflected and determined. He reflected (فَكَّرَ) upon what he imagined as a slander against the Qur'an, and determined (وَقَدَّرَ) within himself what he would say about it.

** فَقُتِلَ كَيْفَ قَدَّرَ ** (19) Perish he! How he determined! An expression of astonishment at his determination.

** ثُمَّ قُتِلَ كَيْفَ قَدَّرَ ** (20) Again, perish he! How he determined! A repetition for emphasis, and ثُمَّ (then/again) indicates that the second instance is more emphatic than the first.

** ثُمَّ نَظَرَ ** (21) Then he looked. Meaning, at the matter of the Qur'an once again.

** ثُمَّ عَبَسَ ** (22) Then he frowned. He knitted his brow, then when he found no slander in it and did not know what to say, and scowled (وَبَسَرَ), which follows عَبَسَ (frowned).

** ثُمَّ أَدْبَرَ ** (23) Then he turned his back. From the truth, and acted arrogantly (وَاسْتَكْبَرَ) regarding following it.

** فَقالَ إِنْ هذا إِلَّا سِحْرٌ يُؤْثَرُ ** (24) Then he said, 'This is nothing but a magic narrated [from others].' That is narrated and learned.

** إِنْ هذا إِلَّا قَوْلُ الْبَشَرِ (25) 'This is nothing but the speech of a human.' Al-Qummi says: It was revealed concerning al-Walid ibn al-Mughirah. He was an old, experienced man, one of the shrewd men (دهاة) of the Arabs, and he was among those who mocked the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. The Messenger of Allah used to sit in the Hijr (the semi-circular wall near the Ka'bah) and recite the Qur'an. So, Quraysh gathered around al-Walid ibn al-Mughirah and said: "O Abu Abd Shams, what is this that Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله is saying? Is it poetry, or soothsaying (كهانة), or a sermon (خطب)?" He said: "Let me hear his speech." So, he approached the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and said: "O Muhammad, recite some of your poetry to me." He said: "It is not poetry, but it is the Speech of Allah, which His angels, prophets, and messengers have approved." He said: "Recite something of it to me." So, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله recited Ha Mim al-Sajdah to him. When He reached His saying: If they turn away (فَإِنْ أَعْرَضُوا), O Muhammad, [meaning] Quraysh, then say (فَقُلْ) to them: 'I warn you of a thunderbolt like the thunderbolt of 'Ad and Thamud.' He said: Al-Walid shuddered, and every hair on his head and beard stood up. He went to his house and did not return to Quraysh that day. So, they went to Abu Jahl and said: "O Abu al-Hakam, Abu Abd Shams has inclined (صبا) toward the religion of Muhammad. Do you not see that he has not returned to us?" So, Abu Jahl went to al-Walid the next morning and said to him: "O uncle, you have lowered our heads, disgraced us, made our enemy rejoice over us, and inclined (صَبَوْتَ) toward the religion of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله." He said: "I have not inclined toward his religion, but I heard from him difficult speech (كلاماً صعباً) that makes the skins shudder." Abu Jahl asked him: "Is it a sermon (خطب)?" He said: "No. A sermon is continuous speech (كلام متّصل), but this is scattered speech (كلام منثور), and parts of it do not resemble other parts." He asked: "Is it poetry?" He said: "No. Indeed, I have heard the poetry of the Arabs—its Basit, its Madid, its Ramal, and its Rajaz—and this is not poetry." He asked: "Then what is it?" He said: "Let me think about it." When it was the next day, they said to him: "O Abu Abd Shams, what do you say about what we discussed?" He said: "Say that it is magic, for it has captivated the hearts of the people." So, Allah revealed concerning that to His Messenger: Leave Me and him whom I created alone (ذَرْنِي وَمَنْ خَلَقْتُ وَحِيداً). He was only named Wahid (alone/unique) because he said to Quraysh: "I alone (أنا أتوحّد) will clothe the Ka'bah one year, and you, in your group, [will clothe it] the next year." He had much wealth and gardens. He had ten sons in Mecca, and he had ten slaves, each of whom had a thousand dinars with which he traded.

In al-Jawami', it is narrated: Al-Walid said to Banu Makhzum: "By Allah, I have just heard speech from Muhammad that is neither the speech of humans nor the speech of jinn. Indeed, it possesses a sweetness, and indeed, it possesses a freshness (طلاوة). Its upper part is fruitful (لمثمر), and its lower part is abundant (لمغدق). And indeed, it rises high and nothing rises above it." Quraysh said: "By Allah, Walid has inclined (صبا). Quraysh will surely incline!" So, Abu Jahl said to him: "I will deal with him for you." He sat with him, appearing sad, and spoke to him with words that angered him. So, he stood up and came to them and said: "You claim that Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله is mad, but have you ever seen him being choked? And you say that he is a soothsayer (كاهن), but have you ever seen him speaking what soothsayers speak? And you claim that he is a poet, but have you ever seen him engaging in poetry? And you claim that he is a liar, but have you ever experienced any lie from him?" They replied concerning all of that: "O Allah, no." They said to him: "Then what is he?" So, he thought and said: "He is nothing but a sorcerer. Do you not see that he separates a man from his wife, his child, and his allies (مواليه)? And what he says is magic narrated from the people of Babylon." So, they dispersed, astonished by him.

In another narration by al-Qummi from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning Umar regarding his denial of the Wilayah (guardianship), and that he was only named Wahid (alone/unique) because he was born out of wedlock (ولد زنا). Then, the verses were interpreted concerning him.

(٢٦) سَأُصْلِيهِ سَقَرَ (١) (26) Soon I shall cast him into Saqar. (٢٧) وَما أَدْراكَ ما سَقَرُ ** (27) And what will show you what Saqar is? A magnification of its affair. (٢٨) لا تُبْقِي وَلا تَذَرُ ** (28) It spares nothing and leaves nothing, It does not spare anything that is cast into it, nor does it leave it until it destroys it. (٢٩) لَوَّاحَةٌ لِلْبَشَرِ ** (29) scorching the skin. Blackening the upper parts of the skin. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, in Hell (جهنّم) there is a valley for the arrogant (للمتكبّرين) called سَقَرُ. It complained to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), about its heat and asked Him to permit it to breathe. So, it breathed, and it burned Hell (جهنّم). And in Rawdat al-Wa'izin, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, in Hell (جهنّم) there is a mountain called صعود (Su'ud), and indeed, in صعود there is a valley called سَقَرُ ** (Saqar), and indeed, in سقر there is a pit (جبّاً) called هبهب (Habhāb). Whenever the cover of that pit is removed, the inhabitants of the Fire (أهل النار) cry out from its heat, and those are the abodes (منازل) of the tyrants (الجبّارين).

(٣٠) عَلَيْها تِسْعَةَ عَشَرَ ** (30) Upon it are nineteen angels (ملائكة) who manage its affair. Al-Qummi said: For every man, there are nineteen angels (ملائكة) who torment him. (٣١) وَما جَعَلْنا أَصْحابَ النَّارِ إِلَّا مَلائِكَةً ** (31) We have appointed none but angels as the guardians of the Fire, so that they may differ from the species of the tormented, thus having no compassion for them and feeling no familiarity (يَسْتَرُوحُونَ) towards them, and because they are the strongest of creation in might (بَأْساً) and the most intense in anger for Allah. It is narrated: When Abu Jahl heard ** عَلَيْها تِسْعَةَ عَشَرَ (Upon it are nineteen), he said to Quraysh: "Are ten men among you incapable of overpowering one man among them?" So, the following was revealed: ** وَما جَعَلْنا عِدَّتَهُمْ إِلَّا فِتْنَةً لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ** (31) and We have appointed their number only as a trial for the faithless, And We appointed their number only as the number that necessitated their trial (فِتْنَة), which is the nineteen. It was said that their being tried (افْتِتَانُهُمْ) by it was due to their deeming it few (اسْتِقْلَالُهُمْ), their mockery, and their considering it unlikely that this small number would undertake the torment of the majority of the two weighty ones (الثقلين - humans and jinn). ** لِيَسْتَيْقِنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتابَ ** (31) so that those who were given the Book may be certain, meaning, so that they may acquire certainty (اليقين) in the prophethood of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the truthfulness of the Quran when they saw that this was consistent with what was in their Book. In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him): They become certain that Allah, His Messenger, and His successor (وَصِيّه) are true. وَ يَزْدادَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِيماناً (31) and the faithful may increase in faith, through belief in Him or through the confirmation of Him by the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب). ** وَلا يَرْتابَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتابَ وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ ** (31) and those who were given the Book and the faithful may not be skeptical, meaning, concerning that (the number nineteen). This is an affirmation (تَأْكِيد) of the certainty (الاسْتِيقَان) and the increase of faith (زِيَادَة الإِيمَان), and a negation of what affects the certain person whenever a doubt (شُبْهَة) afflicts him. وَ لِيَقُولَ الَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ مَرَضٌ (31) and so that those in whose hearts is a sickness meaning doubt (شَكّ) or hypocrisy (نِفَاق), وَالْكافِرُونَ (31) and the faithless meaning those who are resolute in denial (التَّكْذِيب) ما ذا أَرادَ اللهُ بِهذا مَثَلاً (31) may say, 'What did Allah mean by this parable?' meaning: What thing did He intend by this strange number (الْعَدَد الْمُسْتَغْرَب), which is as strange as a parable (اسْتِغْرَاب الْمَثَل)? ** كَذلِكَ يُضِلُّ اللهُ مَنْ يَشاءُ وَيَهْدِي مَنْ يَشاءُ وَما يَعْلَمُ جُنُودَ رَبِّكَ ** (31) Thus does Allah lead astray whomever He wishes, and guide whomever He wishes. And none knows the hosts of your Lord meaning the types (أَصْنَاف) of His creation as they truly are ** إِلَّا هُوَ وَما هِيَ ** (31) except Him. And it It was said that 'it' (هِيَ) refers to Saqar (سَقَر), or the number of the keepers (عِدَّة الْخَزَنَة), or the Surah (السُّورَة). In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him), He said: It means the guardianship (وِلَايَة) of Ali (peace be upon him). ** إِلَّا ذِكْرى لِلْبَشَرِ ** (31) is nothing but a reminder for the mortals. except a reminder (تَذْكِرَة) for them.

footnote (١) أي سأدخله جهنم وألزمه إياها ، وقيل : سقر دركة من دركاتها. (1) Meaning: I shall make him enter Hell (جهنم) and make him cling to it. And it was said: Saqar (سَقَر) is one of its levels (دَرَكَات).

** كَلَّا ** (No, indeed!) is a deterrent for whoever denied it, or a denial that they might remember by means of it. وَالْقَمَرِ. (and the moon,) ** وَاللَّيْلِ إِذْ أَدْبَرَ ** (and the night when it retreats,) دبر is in the meaning of أدبر, like قبل is in the meaning of اقبل, meaning it departed and passed away. And it was said that دبر means it came following the day. And it was recited as إذ أدبر from الأدبار. ** وَالصُّبْحِ إِذا أَسْفَرَ ** (and the dawn when it brightens,) meaning, it shone. ** إِنَّها لَإِحْدَى الْكُبَرِ ** (it is indeed one of the great things,) meaning, one of the great calamities (البلايا الكبر). In the preceding narration: He (the Imam) said: (الولاية). ** نَذِيراً لِلْبَشَرِ ** (a warning to the humans,) meaning, an admonition (إنذار) for them, or a warner (منذرة). ** لِمَنْ شاءَ مِنْكُمْ أَنْ يَتَقَدَّمَ أَوْ يَتَأَخَّرَ ** (for whoever of you wishes to advance or lag behind.) So that he may advance toward good or lag behind it. He said in the preceding narration: Whoever advanced toward our Guardianship (ولايتنا) was kept back from Saqar, and whoever lagged behind it advanced toward Saqar. ** كُلُّ نَفْسٍ بِما كَسَبَتْ رَهِينَةٌ ** (Every soul is a pledge for what it has earned,) meaning, pledged with Allah. ** إِلَّا أَصْحابَ الْيَمِينِ ** (except the People of the Right Hand,) for they have freed their necks by the good deeds they performed. In the preceding narration: By Allah, they are our followers (شيعتنا). And Al-Qummi said: الْيَمِينِ ** (the Right) refers to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), and his companions are his followers (شيعته). ** فِي جَنَّاتٍ يَتَساءَلُونَ (in gardens, they will question) ** عَنِ الْمُجْرِمِينَ ** (concerning the guilty ones:) Some of them will question others, or they will question others about their condition, like your saying: تداعيناه (we called upon him repeatedly), meaning دعوناه (we called upon him). ** ما سَلَكَكُمْ فِي سَقَرَ ** ('What landed you in Saqar?') A narration of what transpired between those being questioned or the guilty ones. ** قالُوا لَمْ نَكُ مِنَ الْمُصَلِّينَ ** (They will say, 'We were not among those who prayed,') It was said that this means the obligatory prayer. In Nahj al-Balaghah: Attend to the matter of prayer, safeguard it, increase in it, and draw near (to Allah) through it, for it كانَتْ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ كِتاباً مَوْقُوتاً ** (is indeed a timed ordinance for the faithful). Do you not listen to the response of the people of the Fire when they were asked: ما سَلَكَكُمْ فِي سَقَرَ قالُوا لَمْ نَكُ مِنَ الْمُصَلِّينَ. ('What landed you in Saqar?' They will say, 'We were not among those who prayed,') And in Al-Kafi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): the like of it. And narrated from Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: It means: We were not among the followers of the Imams, those concerning whom Allah said: وَالسَّابِقُونَ السَّابِقُونَ أُولئِكَ الْمُقَرَّبُونَ ** (And the foremost ones are the foremost ones: they are the brought near [to Allah]). Do you not see that people call the one who follows the foremost in the race (الحلبة) a مصلّياً (a follower)? That is what is meant where He said: لَمْ نَكُ مِنَ الْمُصَلِّينَ , meaning: We were not among the followers of the foremost ones (السابقين). And narrated from Al-Kazim (peace be upon him), He said: It means that we did not take as guardian (نتولّ) the successor (وصيّ) of Muhammad and the successors after him (peace be upon them), and we did not pray for them.

(٤٤) وَلَمْ نَكُ نُطْعِمُ الْمِسْكِينَ ** (44) and we did not feed the needy. (what must be given to him). Al-Qummi said: [It refers to] the rights of the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) from the Khums (الخمس) designated for the relatives (ذوي القربى), the orphans (اليتامى), the needy (المساكين), and the traveler (ابن السبيل), and they are the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them).

(٤٥)** وَكُنَّا نَخُوضُ مَعَ الْخائِضِينَ ** (45) We used to indulge in vain talk along with those who indulged. (We embark upon falsehood along with those who embark upon it).

(٤٦) وَكُنَّا نُكَذِّبُ بِيَوْمِ الدِّينِ ** (46) and we used to deny the Day of Retribution, Meaning: And after all of that, we were deniers of the Resurrection (القيامة) and its postponement, due to its magnitude (لتعظيمه).

(٤٧) حَتَّى أَتانَا الْيَقِينُ ** (47) until the certainty came to us.’ (Death (الموت)).

(٤٨)** فَما تَنْفَعُهُمْ شَفاعَةُ الشَّافِعِينَ ** (48) So the intercession of the intercessors will not benefit them. (Even if they all interceded for them).

(٤٩) فَما لَهُمْ عَنِ التَّذْكِرَةِ مُعْرِضِينَ. (49) What is the matter with them that they turn away from the Reminder? In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him), He said: "Meaning: turning away from the Guardianship (الولاية)." And Al-Qummi said: "From what is mentioned to them concerning the allegiance (موالاة) to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him)."

(٥٠) كَأَنَّهُمْ حُمُرٌ مُسْتَنْفِرَةٌ. (50) as if they were startled asses,

(٥١) فَرَّتْ مِنْ قَسْوَرَةٍ ** (51) fleeing from a lion. (He likened them in their turning away (إعراضهم) and their aversion (نفارهم) from listening to the Reminder (الذكر) to startled asses fleeing from a lion (أسد)).

(٥٢) بَلْ يُرِيدُ كُلُّ امْرِئٍ مِنْهُمْ أَنْ يُؤْتى صُحُفاً مُنَشَّرَةً ** (52) Rather, every one of them desires to be given open scrolls. (Scrolls (قراطيس) that are spread open and read). It was said that this is because they said to the Prophet (may the peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his Family): "We will not follow you until you bring each one of us a book from the heaven containing [a message] from Allah to so-and-so: 'Follow Muhammad (may the peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his Family).'" And Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "That is because they said: 'O Muhammad, we have been informed that a man from the Children of Israel (بني إسرائيل) would commit a sin, and when he awoke, his sin would be written beside his head, along with its expiation (كفّارته).' Then Gabriel (peace be upon him) descended upon the Messenger of Allah (may the peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his Family) and said: 'Your people are asking you for the practice (سنّة) of the Children of Israel regarding sins. If they wish, We shall do that to them and hold them accountable in the manner We used to hold the Children of Israel accountable.' They claimed that the Messenger of Allah (may the peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his Family) disliked that for his people."

(٥٣) كَلَّا ** (53) No indeed! A deterrent against their proposal of signs (الآيات). Rather, they do not fear the Hereafter, and for that reason, they turn away from the reminder (التذكرة).

(٥٤) كَلَّا ** (54) No indeed! A deterrent against their turning away. It is a reminder**, and what a reminder it is!

(٥٥) فَمَنْ شاءَ ذَكَرَهُ. (55) So let anyone who wishes remember it.

(٥٦) وَما يَذْكُرُونَ إِلَّا أَنْ يَشاءَ اللهُ ** (56) But they will not remember unless Allah wishes. And it was recited with the tā’ (بالتاء). He is worthy of being feared (or: He is worthy of piety), meaning, He is deserving that His punishment be feared (يُتَّقَى عِقَابُهُ). And worthy of forgiveness**, meaning, He is deserving that He forgive His servants.

In al-Tawhid, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, he said: Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, said: "I am worthy that I be feared (أَهْلٌ أَنْ أُتَّقَى), and that My servant does not associate anything with Me. And I am worthy that if My servant does not associate anything with Me, I admit him into Paradise (الجنّة)." And he (peace be upon him) said: "Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, swore by His Might (عزّته) and His Majesty (جلاله) that He would never punish the people of His Oneness (أهل توحيده) with the Fire."

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "Whoever recites Surat al-Muddaththir in the obligatory prayer (الفريضة), it is a right upon Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, that He place him with Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم in His rank (درجته), and no misery (شقاء) shall ever reach him in the life of this world, if Allah wills."
